title: ACI Fault Domain Severity Rollup
exclusions: Non-ACI **Nexus** fabrics (**NDFC** standalone without **APIC** fault MO families); **MSO/NDFC** orchestration faults that never hit leaf **faultInst**; pure **ITSI** service-aggregates that do not preserve **dn** granularity; duplicate coverage when UC-18.1.11 already drives per-**faultInst** lifecycle pacing for the same object list.
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) with five complementary sourcetypes: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** (**Managed Object** pulls of **`faultInst`**, optional **`faultDelegate`**, and hourly **`faultSummary.json`** rollups from **`https://<apic>/api/class/faultSummary.json`** plus node-scoped **`https://<apic>/api/node/class/faultInst.json`** when **`query-target-filter`** excludes **cleared** rows), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** (**APIC** mnemonic strings that mirror **`flt`** codes during soak storms), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**aaaModLR** edits that explain policy bursts), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** (**pod**/**APIC** health scorecards), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:tenant_config`** (**fvTenant**/**fvBD** intent snapshots for tenant-domain context) — typical REST cadence **120–300s** for MO classes, **60–300s** for health.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**Splunkbase** **4022**) for **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, and parser macros; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, **Splunkbase** **1546**) authenticated through **`/api/aaaLogin.json`** then **`/api/aaaRefresh.json`** for **`faultInst`**, **`faultSummary`**, and config streams; **Cisco DC Networking for Splunk Platform** (**Splunkbase** **7777**) for starter dashboards once indexes and **FIELDALIAS** mappings match this UC.
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval lc_s=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,fault_lc,lifecycle,lifecycleState,"")))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,faultSeverity,"")))
| eval code_u=upper(trim(coalesce(code,fault_code,faultCode,faultId,"")))
| eval dn_k=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,fault_dn,"")))
| eval apic_id=lower(trim(coalesce(apic,apic_name,apicCluster,host,"fabric")))
| eval delegated_f=lower(trim(coalesce(delegated,delegation,delegatedFlag,"no")))
| eval fault_domain=coalesce(lower(trim(faultDomain)),lower(trim(domain)),null())
| eval fault_domain=if(isnull(fault_domain) OR fault_domain="",case(match(dn_k,"(^|/)aaa(user|domain)|^uni/user|mgmtgrp"),"mgmt",match(dn_k,"(^|/)tn-[^/]+|/ctx-[^/]+|/ap-[^/]+|/epg-|^uni/tn-"),"tenant",match(dn_k,"(^|/)phys/|l1ports|l2ports|if-|cdpctl|fabricpath|accport"),"access",match(dn_k,"(^|/)topology/|(^|/)fabric/|fabric(out|in)|bgpinst|ospfinst|isisdom"),"fabric",match(dn_k,"(^|/)infra/|(^|/)pol-(obj-)?|(^|/)nwss|(^|/)fabricinst"),"infra",true(),"unknown"),fault_domain)
| eval is_cleared=if(sev="cleared" OR lc_s IN ("cleared","resolved"),1,0)
| eval is_open=if(is_cleared=1 OR isnull(sev) OR sev="",0,1)
| where is_open=1 AND match(sev,"^(critical|major|minor|warning)$") AND (delegated_f IN ("no","false","0","") OR isnull(delegated_f))
| stats dc(dn_k) as affected_mos dc(code_u) as distinct_codes dc(apic_id) as apic_footprint max(len(code_u)) as code_width by fault_domain sev
| eventstats sum(affected_mos) as fabric_total
| eval pct_of_open=round(100*affected_mos/fabric_total,2)
| sort fault_domain -affected_mos -sev
| eval join_key="rollup_v1"
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:health" earliest=-2h latest=now
    | eval join_key="rollup_v1"
    | head 1
    | eval health_feed_alive=1
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" earliest=-2h latest=now
    | eval join_key="rollup_v1"
    | head 1
    | eval syslog_feed_alive=1
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" earliest=-2h latest=now
    | eval join_key="rollup_v1"
    | head 1
    | eval audit_feed_alive=1
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:tenant_config" earliest=-2h latest=now
    | eval join_key="rollup_v1"
    | head 1
    | eval tenant_cfg_feed_alive=1
  ]
| table fault_domain sev affected_mos distinct_codes apic_footprint pct_of_open health_feed_alive syslog_feed_alive audit_feed_alive tenant_cfg_feed_alive
description: Aggregates **Cisco ACI** **faultInst** (and aligned **faultSummary**) rows into **fault domains** — **tenant**, **infra**, **fabric**, **access**, and **mgmt** — while **severity** remains **critical**/**major**/**minor**/**warning**, excluding **cleared** lifecycles so operations sees which functional slice of the policy model currently holds the densest unresolved defects.
value: Executives and duty managers get a **single heat-map narrative** that answers “which neighborhood of the cloth is hurting” instead of sorting thousands of **dn** rows by hand—shortening **bridge calls**, focusing **CHG** rollback decisions, and feeding **problem management** with domain-scoped defect concentration tied to real **MO** coverage.
implementation: Land **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**4022**) feeds and **rest_ta** (**1546**) MO pulls for **faultInst**/**faultSummary** with **FIELDALIAS** symmetry on **lc**, **severity**, **code**, **dn**; derive **fault_domain** from vendor fields when present else **dn** regex; schedule hourly rollups and alert on domain/sev density; cross-check **7777** dashboard drilldowns.
visualization: **Stacked column** chart (**fault_domain** × **severity** with **affected_mos**), **sorted table** for **pct_of_open**, **single-value** badges for **critical**/**major** totals, **heatmap** optional for **compare** across **apic_footprint**, feed-gate lamps for **health_feed_alive**/**syslog_feed_alive**/**audit_feed_alive**/**tenant_cfg_feed_alive**.
schema: **Alerts** alignment maps **severity**, **signature** (**code_u**), **src** (**apic_id**), and **dest** (**dn_k**); **Change** alignment tags bursts with **aaaModLR** IDs ingested on **`cisco:aci:audit`**.

references.title: Cisco APIC Faults, Events, and System Messages Management Guide
Cisco APIC Object Model Reference — faultInst
Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **APIC** clusters on supported 6.x trains must expose HTTPS 443/tcp to the Heavy Forwarder or **rest_ta** host; keep RBAC personas read-only with aaa refresh tokens isolated from interactive admins so Splunk polling does not share rotating shared-secret keyboards.
• Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([**Splunkbase** **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) on the syslog receiver that ingests **APIC** AAA, FLT, and AUDIT classes — retain distinct sourcetype lanes (**`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**) so parser upgrades roll back cleanly.
• Deploy **rest_ta** ([**Splunkbase** **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) or an approved Python modular collector that logs in with **`POST /api/aaaLogin.json`**, maintains **`POST /api/aaaRefresh.json`** roughly every 600s, and enumerates **`faultInst`** via **`/api/node/class/faultInst.json`** (scoped per **APIC** node) plus optional **`/api/class/faultSummary.json`** for pre-aggregated domain/severity tuples when your automation already trusts **APIC** rollups.
• Map DNS PTR and CMDB columns onto apic/host so joins between fault feeds and health feeds line up when multiple clusters land in one index.
• Size index volumes: a 600 EPG fabric can emit 15k–80k faultInst evals per day during storms — place **`cisco_aci`** on warm tiers with 15–30 day hot retention for triage.
• Optional: mirror **Cisco DC Networking** dashboards ([**Splunkbase** **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) only after **`cisco_aci`** macros match this UC; misaligned macros create false empty panels.

• License/retention: budget **`cisco_aci`** at ~0.5–1.5 KB per indexed **faultInst** (after TA parsing); scale by REST cadence, concurrent opens, hot retention, plus ~25% slack for ISSU/policy bursts that inflate chatter before **APIC** publishes **critical** MO faults on traffic-bearing paths.
• Credentials: dedicated APIC reader (CAB-scoped), Splunk secret storage only, rotate with TLS trust refresh on rest_ta collectors—never shared admin passwords on forwarders.



Step 1 Configure data collection
• Create **rest_ta** stanzas (or modular input equivalents) that pull **`faultInst`** with **`rsp-subtree`** set to no for leaf objects and **`query-target-filter`** that excludes **`faultInst.lc=="cleared"`** when you want streaming “open fault” snapshots — mirror the Visore pattern **`and(gt(faultInst.severity,"cleared"))`** only if your **APIC** release maps severity ordinals that way; otherwise filter on **`lc`** fields in Splunk for honesty.
• Add a second stanza for **`faultSummary`** (`**/api/class/faultSummary.json**`) if you prefer **APIC**-side counting; keep both raw **`faultInst`** and summary lanes during migration so dashboards do not flip counts overnight.
• Enable syslog forwarding of F#### mnemonics to **`cisco:aci:syslog`** for sub-minute correlation when REST cadence is 180s — tag **`host`** with the **APIC** node name that emitted the event.
• Keep **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`** streams (contract snapshot or fvTenant JSON) on a slower cadence (300–900s) strictly for tenant-domain tie-breaks — never substitute config snapshots for fault truth.
• On **`props.conf`**, normalize **`TIME_PREFIX`**, **`MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD`**, and **`LINE_BREAKER`** for multi-line JSON batches; add **`FIELDALIAS-severity`** / **`FIELDALIAS-lc`** stanzas so **`severity`**, **`lc`**, **`code`**, **`dn`** line up across TA versions.
• Record baseline documentation: expected objects/pod, change windows, and ISSU plan — domain rollups spike during maintenance and need CHG labels for suppression stores.

Step 2 Create the search
Run the SPL from the **`spl`** field in Search, then save as a scheduled report and a threshold alert after validation.

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval lc_s=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,fault_lc,lifecycle,lifecycleState,"")))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,faultSeverity,"")))
| ...derive fault_domain and stats...
```

Understanding this SPL
• Scope — **`index=cisco_aci`** and **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** anchor the rollup on MO truth; syslog is not trusted for counts here, only for corroboration in Step 3.
• Lifecycle — **`lc_s`** and **`sev`** gate **cleared** rows; adjust `match` if your TA emits `raised-clearing` states you want to treat as still open.
• Delegation — the **`delegated`** filter drops parent bubble rows that would double-count fabric defects when children already list identical **`dn`** paths.
• Domains — `case` mirrors **APIC** fault domains: `tenant` when **`dn`** references `/tn-`, `fabric` for `topology/` / routing classes, `access` for `phys` / port classes, `infra` for `infra/` / policy plumbing, `mgmt` for aaa users — extend `match` with `eqpt` patterns if your MIM revision exposes new branches.
• Stats — `dc(dn_k)` counts distinct managed objects, not events — closer to Visore than raw sum(count) across polls.
• Percent narrative — **`pct_of_open`** highlights concentration; pair with **`apic_footprint`** to spot domains that are wide but shallow versus narrow and deep.
• Parallel feed proof — `join` subsearches against **`cisco:aci:health`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`** set **`*_feed_alive`** flags; a 0 tells you the rollup may be blind due to collector outages, not calm fabrics.

Step 3 Validate
• Open **APIC** Faults UI, filter unresolved **critical**/**major**, and for each domain bucket compare object counts to **`affected_mos`** — tolerate ±10% when REST polling lags syslog by one interval.
• Spot-check three random **`dn_k`** values per domain: confirm **`code_u`** matches **APIC** code, **`lc_s`** still raised, and delegation flags align.
• Run `| stats count by sourcetype` on **`index=cisco_aci`** for the same window — verify all five sourcetypes show non-zero bytes unless intentionally disabled.
• Compare **`faultSummary`** JSON (if enabled) to Splunk — drift indicates **FIELDALIAS** gaps or double ingestion of both summary and inst without dedup.
• During CHG, annotate Observed/Expected columns in your workbook so future reviewers know a spike was authorized.

• Delegate drift: `index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-24h | fillnull value="" delegated | stats dc(dn_k) as mos values(delegated) as dval` — dval should read empty/false/no; true-heavy splits mean parent **faultInst** rows bypass **`delegated`** gating or faultDelegate feeds still need deduping before KPIs.
• Tenant ordering: tenant `top` **`dn_k`** ordering should mirror APIC Faults that hour—parallel code_u with scrambled ranks signals FIELDALIAS drift, not new contracts.
• PHY ↔ syslog: ±15m around PHY work, compare **`cisco:aci:fault`** access counts to `cisco:aci:syslog` F#### bursts; quiet REST + loud syslog usually means query-target-filter removed warnings you still track.
• Feed cadence: 5m `tstats` on `sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault` must track **`cisco:aci:health`**; fault silence while health moves implies stuck **rest_ta** or HTTP brownouts, not fabric quiet.



Step 4 Operationalize
• Publish a dashboard with role-based read access for NOC and write for automation owners; embed drilldowns to **`dn_k`** detail searches and Visore deep links stored in **`references`**.
• Wire lookup tables **`aci_chg_calendar.csv`** (change id, window, owner) and join on _time to auto-tag maintenance-inflated domains.
• Export daily CSV snapshots to problem teams — domain/severity matrices age better than flat event dumps.
• Feed summary index (`summary` index or KV store) with **`outputlookup`** if executives need 90-day trends without rescans.
• Document suppression logic referencing **`knownFalsePositives`** tokens so analysts know why a muted page fired historically.

Step 5 Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Visualization layout — Top row: single-value tiles for `critical` and `major` totals with sparkline timechart underneath showing **`affected_mos`** per **`fault_domain`**. Middle: stacked column chart with **`severity`** split-by colors consistent across `tenant`, `fabric`, `access`; add reference lines for yesterday’s totals. Bottom: table sorted on **`pct_of_open`** with **`distinct_codes`** truncated to three entries plus “+N” hint. Side lane: feed alive LED panels sourced from **`*_feed_alive`** fields; color red when 0 for more than two consecutive checks. Offer a drilldown modal launching **`makeresults | eval dn_k=$click.value$`** pattern searches that join **`cisco:aci:audit`** for recent editors.

Alert design — Schedule every 15 minutes; use custom condition `search af fected_mos > 25 AND sev IN ("critical","major") AND fault_domain!="unknown"` for sev-2, tighten to >40 for sev-1. Add throttle: 30m per **`fault_domain`**, **`sev`** tuple. Route email + webhook payloads listing top three **`code_u`**, **`apic_footprint`**, and `health_feed_ok`. Include recovery alert when **`affected_mos`** drops below baseline for one hour to clear PagerDuty. For maintenance, reference **`inputlookup`** suppress keyed by `CHG`.

Failure modes — Empty dashboards usually mean `sourcetype` typo (**`cisco:aci:faults`**) or index permissions; exploded counts mean delegate filter disabled; all domains `unknown` means **`dn_k`** blank — fix TA LINE_BREAKER; `*_feed_alive`=0 means collector host lost DNS or certificate trust — rotate serverCert pinning; percent totals drift when **cleared** faults still count — revisit **`lc_s`** mapping for `retaining` states. **APIC** HTTP 401 storms: refresh aaa tokens or split poll intervals; HTTP 503: leader election — correlate with **`cisco:aci:health`** role fields.

• Contract/LPG infra waves: tie spikes to `cisco:aci:audit` sessions; escalate **major** scenarios only when fabric and access leaf pairs worsen together—infra-only surges often finish after profiler/LPG settles without dataplane hits.
• Mgmt/TACACS load: page tenant/access faultInst bursts ahead of aaa-only mgmt churn; keep **critical** alerts on codes tied to observed loss, not credential rotations alone.
• Fabric warnings + loss KPIs: hand-review access faultInst (vpc, l1PhysIf MO paths) before clearing spines—PHY defects routinely mis-file into the wrong fault_domain bucket.
• HTTP 503/429 storms: temporarily widen **rest_ta** polling, brief teams with `cisco:aci:syslog`, correlate timestamps to leader/role churn in **`cisco:aci:health`** so rollups stay honest during brownouts.
grandmaExplanation: We sort live trouble tickets by whether they hit tenant spaces, shared wiring, fabric spines, rack ports, or the admin knobs—so the messiest corner gets help first without reading a novel of codes.
hardware: Cisco **APIC** cluster, Cisco **Nexus** 9000 switches in **ACI** mode
knownFalsePositives:
**fault_domain_rollup_delegate_bias** — Temporarily toggling **delegate** visibility or duplicating **faultDelegate** pulls alongside **faultInst** can make **parent** domains look heavier than **child** MOs until **Splunk** filters align—require **`delegated=no`** gating (as in the primary SPL) and verify **Visore** shows the same **MO** cardinality before paging.

**visore_cleared_apic_lag** — **APIC** **GUI** may mark **cleared** milliseconds before **`lc`** columns land in **REST** rows, so **Splunk** still counts **open** faults briefly—blend **`cisco:aci:syslog`** **CLEAR** mnemonics or delay **alerts** **90s** after **CHG** completion unless **codes** remain **raised** in **both** transports.

**maintenance_flood_domain_inflation** — **ISSU**, **LLPG** rebuilds, or **full** **policy** **repushes** legitimately concentrate **minor**/**warning** defects in **infra**/**fabric** lanes—tie **Splunk** timelines to **`cisco:aci:audit`** **session** IDs and **`aci_chg_calendar`** lookups before treating spikes as regressions.

**dn_prefix_misclassification** — Custom **naming** that flattens **tenant** paths into **l3extOut** DNs can push **tenant-looking** defects into **fabric** buckets—maintain a **`lookup aci_dn_domain_overrides.csv`** keyed by **`regex`** and **`forced_domain`** when automation strips **`/tn-`** markers.

**fault_summary_poll_coarsening** — **`faultSummary.json`** aggregates faster than per-**MO** pulls but hides **delegation** nuances—if dashboards mix **summary** and **inst** without **`dedup`**, **access**/**fabric** ratios stretch—standardize on one authoritative stream per panel.

**multi_apic_duplicate_fault_rows** — Dual **collectors** hitting the same **APIC** with different **host** headers can double **event** volume—tag **`inputs.conf`** **stanzas** uniquely and **`dedup _time dn_k code_u`** when migrating inputs.

**lifecycle_transient_minor_churn** — **Soaking** timers elevate **minor** codes that **clear** automatically after **TCAM** compilation—use **15m** rolling **average** gates for **minor**/**warning** alerts while keeping **hard** thresholds for **major**/**critical**.

**tenant_config_echo_only_faults** — **fvTenant** snapshot gaps sometimes surface “fake” emptiness: **tenant** domain looks quiet while **syslog** screams—never silence **tenant** lanes using only **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`** freshness; require **`cisco:aci:fault`** corroboration.

**health_snapshot_domain_mismatch** — **Health** scores can stay **green** while **policy** defects accumulate in **tenant**/**infra** domains—treat **`cisco:aci:health`** as **telemetry**, not proof of **fault** absence when **`health_feed_alive=1` but `affected_mos` high`**. Pair with **code** list drilldowns.
